# baseten_custom_model (Resource)

Deploys a Baseten custom model archive and configures deployment autoscaling.

## Import

Existing deployments can be imported by model and deployment ID:

```shell
terraform import baseten_custom_model.example model-123:deployment-456
```

Keep the resource block configured after import because Baseten cannot
reconstruct local-only inputs such as `source_path`, `source_hash`, and
`config_json`.

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `config_json` (String) Baseten deployment config as a JSON object string.
- `max_replica` (Number)
- `min_replica` (Number) Minimum replica count. Use 0 to allow scale-to-zero.
- `name` (String)
- `source_hash` (String) Caller-provided hash of the local model source. Changing this value replaces the deployment.
- `source_path` (String) Path to the local model directory to archive and upload.

### Optional

- `concurrency_target` (Number)
- `deployment_name` (String)
- `disable_archive_access` (Boolean)
- `environment_name` (String) Baseten environment to push to, such as production, for a stable endpoint.
- `preserve_env_instance_type` (Boolean) When environment_name targets an existing environment, preserve that environment instance type instead of the config instance type.
- `raw_config` (String) Original config.yaml contents to persist with the deployment.
- `scale_down_delay` (Number)

### Read-Only

- `active_replica_count` (Number)
- `deployment_id` (String)
- `deployment_status` (String)
- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
- `model_id` (String)
